Event: SpongeBob prevents the permanent paint from touching Mr. Krabs' prized possessions
Explanation: He expends effort but successfully protects his boss’s valuables with no downside shown.

Event: SpongeBob saves Mr. Krabs from King Neptune by offering to journey to Shell City for the crown
Explanation: He risks his own safety and ultimately succeeds in saving Krabs and the kingdom.

Event: SpongeBob stays a long time in Sandy’s treedome without a helmet
Explanation: His ignorance nearly causes him serious harm while not affecting others.

Event: SpongeBob expects overnight celebrity after obscure Krusty Krab commercial
Explanation: His inflated expectations bring him no benefit and some humiliation.

Event: SpongeBob assumes he’ll be manager of Krusty Krab 2 and humiliates himself on the microphone
Explanation: His overconfidence leads to repeated self-humiliation without clear harm to others.

Event: SpongeBob takes Squidward in when he’s homeless and lets him live there long-term
Explanation: He sacrifices comfort and sanity to house Squidward, who exploits him.

Event: SpongeBob drags Squidward (still in bed) to the Krusty Krab to get his job back
Explanation: He forcefully pushes Squidward to employment, relieving his own burden and restoring Squidward’s job.

Event: SpongeBob snaps and strangles Mr. Krabs while yelling about the dime
Explanation: His outburst risks his job and assaults his boss, only accidentally revealing the dime.

Event: SpongeBob calls out Mr. Krabs for torturing Gary to get loose change
Explanation: He confronts his boss to protect Gary, safeguarding his pet and pushing back against abuse.

Event: SpongeBob explodes in rage when Patrick puts Krabby Patties on his feet
Explanation: He violently overreacts to a petty provocation, harming Patrick with no benefit.

Event: SpongeBob protects Patrick and repeatedly goes through troubles to keep him okay
Explanation: His protective efforts cost him but maintain Patrick’s well-being and their bond.

Event: SpongeBob eats a homemade sundae of ketchup, onions, and a peanut plant
Explanation: He indulges in something that is unpleasant and harmful to himself with no upside.

Event: SpongeBob throws Sandy on the ground to keep waiting for his toy
Explanation: He physically harms a friend over a trivial obsession and gains nothing long-term.

Event: SpongeBob kills many scallops defending the Patty he loves
Explanation: He commits large-scale harm to protect an inanimate object he’s attached to.

Event: SpongeBob tortures Plankton with his laugh at Mr. Krabs’ direction
Explanation: He cooperates in psychological torture, pleasing his boss while badly harming Plankton.

Event: SpongeBob lets a random fish sit on him without protest in “Walking Small”
Explanation: He allows himself to be literally used as furniture to keep peace, hurting only himself.

Event: SpongeBob uses Gary to train in boot-camp style for the Snail Olympics
Explanation: His harsh regimen harms Gary physically for SpongeBob’s competitive pride.

Event: SpongeBob overly seriously trains Gary to win the snail race and overworks him
Explanation: He injures Gary pursuing petty victory and doesn’t even gain the win.

Event: SpongeBob repeatedly fails his driving test over 1 million times trying to get license
Explanation: His persistence causes massive destruction and mental damage to Mrs. Puff with no success.

Event: SpongeBob drives like crazy, running over pedestrians in later episodes
Explanation: His reckless driving repeatedly devastates the city and harms himself via legal/safety risk.

Event: SpongeBob becomes narcissistic after Krusty Krab commercial and neglects reality
Explanation: His ego alienates others and yields no real gains from the trivial fame.

Event: SpongeBob uses ripped-pants gag as he’s “dying” on the beach to grab attention
Explanation: He exploits others’ concern for attention, mildly harming their feelings.

Event: SpongeBob pushes Squidward too far with clinginess in “Breath of Fresh Squidward”
Explanation: His refusal to respect boundaries hurts Squidward and brings no benefit when rejected.

Event: SpongeBob hosts Squidward and Patrick’s “Good Neighbors” attempts, causing chaos
Explanation: His misguided neighborhood help torments Squidward and exhausts himself.

Event: SpongeBob cries and falls into depression when Gary runs away
Explanation: His neglect hurts himself emotionally and others only indirectly.

Event: SpongeBob and friends create a national holiday to get away from him (others’ action)
Explanation: They gain relief from his presence while deeply hurting him by exclusion.

Event: SpongeBob does nothing when Gary is attacked by Puffy Fluffy, blaming Gary
Explanation: His total misperception nearly costs Gary’s life and gives SpongeBob no benefit.

Event: SpongeBob steals Puffy Fluffy and ignores the seller’s warnings
Explanation: He commits theft and endangers his own pet based on gullibility.

Event: SpongeBob indulges as “crazy snail guy” taking in impossible number of stray snails
Explanation: He sacrifices his home and comfort to care for many snails, largely benefiting them.

Event: SpongeBob spends Mr. Krabs’ credit card completely on Pearl’s birthday gifts
Explanation: Pearl’s day is saved but he blows Krabs’ money with no personal gain and risk of backlash.

Event: SpongeBob remodels his house into a replica of Squidward’s to lure him over
Explanation: His obsessive act creeps Squidward out and doesn’t truly improve the relationship.

Event: SpongeBob helps Plankton by mistake in “Plankton!” and “Walking Small”
Explanation: His gullibility furthers villainous schemes while not helping himself.

Event: SpongeBob attempts a panty raid with Patrick and Mr. Krabs in “Mid-Life Crustacean”
Explanation: He joins a voyeuristic break-in for cheap thrills, risking trouble and violating privacy.

Event: SpongeBob fills his house with stored garbage and preserved sweat out of nostalgia
Explanation: His hoarding harms his own living conditions for sentimental reasons.

Event: SpongeBob uses Gary as a drill-sergeant trainee for snail boot camp
Explanation: He stresses Gary for competition, neither clearly winning nor helping anyone.

Event: SpongeBob writes sensationalist lies in “Krabby Kronicle” that ruin lives
Explanation: He gains approval/job performance from Mr. Krabs at significant cost to others’ reputations.

Event: SpongeBob publishes an exposé on Mr. Krabs forcing him to print lies
Explanation: He regains his conscience and shames Krabs, risking his job but restoring some justice.

Event: SpongeBob cooks at the Krusty Krab for love of the job despite low pay
Explanation: He finds fulfillment in work and provides great service, even if underpaid.

Event: SpongeBob overreacts to Plankton’s mild criticism about too much sauce
Explanation: He collapses emotionally from minor critique, hurting only himself.

Event: SpongeBob chooses not to kill the health inspector after serving a tainted patty
Explanation: He abandons Krabs’ murderous plan, protecting a life and his own conscience.

Event: SpongeBob uses his laugh as a torture tool on Plankton under Krabs’ orders
Explanation: He follows orders to inflict severe harm on Plankton without questioning.

Event: SpongeBob stalks Squidward through windows and into his house multiple times
Explanation: His boundary violations distress Squidward and don’t secure genuine friendship.

Event: SpongeBob repeatedly ruins Mrs. Puff’s life with his driving tests
Explanation: His incompetence and obliviousness push her toward insanity while he learns nothing.

Event: SpongeBob scolds Gary instead of feeding him in “Have You Seen This Snail?”
Explanation: His neglect and misdirected anger drive Gary away and harm their bond.

Event: SpongeBob manipulates Patrick as “dad” while he is “mom” to the scallop
Explanation: He pushes Patrick to responsibility, which helps the “family” dynamic even if straining things.

Event: SpongeBob and Patrick terrorize Bikini Bottom as invisible “ghosts” in Pranks a Lot
Explanation: He enjoys pranks and fear at substantial emotional cost to others until payback arrives.

Event: SpongeBob cuts Squidward’s TV cables to force him to attend his party
Explanation: He coerces attendance, damaging property and the relationship without real benefit.

Event: SpongeBob neglects his guests’ fun by enforcing a rigid party schedule
Explanation: His perfectionism ruins everyone’s enjoyment and leaves him socially worse off.

Event: SpongeBob buys a vicious “playmate” for Gary in “A Pal for Gary” despite warnings
Explanation: He ignores clear danger signs, directly endangering his pet with no upsides.

Event: SpongeBob scolds Patrick harshly after being glued into the wringer
Explanation: His anger at Patrick is understandable but he lashes out unproductively and gets blamed.

Event: SpongeBob attempts to steal panties in “Mid-Life Crustacean” with others
Explanation: He participates in a sexualized break-in for kicks at someone else’s expense.

Event: SpongeBob forcibly drags Squidward into group activities despite protests
Explanation: His refusal to take “no” harms Squidward’s peace and doesn’t deepen real friendship.

Event: SpongeBob accepts extreme underpayment / paying to work at Krusty Krab
Explanation: He gives away labor and even money for Krabs’ profit while getting mainly emotional reward.

Event: SpongeBob repeatedly forgives Mr. Krabs’ extreme greed and abuse
Explanation: His refusal to hold Krabs accountable allows continued exploitation and rarely helps others.

Event: SpongeBob misinterprets punishments as fun activities (e.g., Inferno Island prison)
Explanation: His obliviousness lets him avoid psychological harm but validates the abusers’ system.

Event: SpongeBob attempts to boycott Mermaid Man movie for using actors
Explanation: He gains no result and only positions himself against harmless creative choices.

Event: SpongeBob repeatedly spoils Squidward’s attempts to move away or vacation
Explanation: His clinginess wrecks Squidward’s escape plans and doesn’t improve his own life.

Event: SpongeBob confronts Mr. Krabs in “One Coarse Meal” about driving Plankton to suicide
Explanation: He defends a rival’s basic life and ethics, risking friction with his boss for a just cause.

Event: SpongeBob accepts punishment of decades of docked pay with enthusiasm
Explanation: He frames severe exploitation as “best boss ever,” harming only himself financially.

Event: SpongeBob works himself into hallucinations by not sleeping for 43 days
Explanation: His overwork annihilates his mental health without helping others more than usual.

Event: SpongeBob refuses to cook for the Chum Bucket in “Welcome to the Chum Bucket”
Explanation: He holds to his principles, thwarting Plankton and preserving his own values.

Event: SpongeBob sabotages Squidward’s Employee of the Month attempt out of paranoia
Explanation: He fiercely protects his status by harming Squidward’s chances.

Event: SpongeBob hides in Patrick’s rock and imprisons him out of jealousy in “Bummer Vacation”
Explanation: His possessive madness traumatises Patrick and doesn’t get him his job back.

Event: SpongeBob rips Patrick’s license in “Driven to Tears”
Explanation: He vents jealousy by destroying Patrick’s reward and gains only momentary catharsis.

Event: SpongeBob helps Squidward by throwing pizza at rude customer in “Pizza Delivery”
Explanation: Squidward’s act defends SpongeBob’s effort; SpongeBob gains affirmation and the bully is checked.

Event: SpongeBob delivers Krusty Krab pizza through hardships without eating it
Explanation: He upholds duty over hunger, showing integrity and satisfying the job’s goal.

Event: SpongeBob confronts Mr. Krabs in “Krusty Love” for abusing money-control arrangement
Explanation: He finally snaps at unfair blame, reasserting boundaries though risking his job.

Event: SpongeBob harshly trains Gary just to beat Squidward’s snail in “Great Snail Race”
Explanation: His jealousy-driven regimen harms Gary and doesn’t secure victory.

Event: SpongeBob imitates Squidward’s cruelty in “Breath of Fresh Squidward” by kicking him out
Explanation: He repeats the same insensitive behavior he hates when roles reverse.

Event: SpongeBob helps mass-evacuate Hotel Halibut in “Jolly Lodgers” after infestation
Actor: Squidward (via pest call); secondary: SpongeBob and Patrick
Explanation: The trio’s actions ultimately get everyone to safety, despite prior annoyance.
